steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence uses a parallel structure: paleoanthropologists can "not only ______ steps in the evolution of hominids but also illuminate the Pleistocene epoch." The blank needs a verb that fits naturally with "steps in evolution" and pairs logically with "illuminate" (to reveal or clarify).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'yield': "yield steps" means to produce or generate steps, but scientists studying fossils discover or recognize steps — they don't produce them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'exploit': "exploit steps" means to take advantage of steps, which doesn't make sense in the context of scientific understanding. ✗
  • Choice C — 'prioritize': "prioritize steps" means to rank them by importance, but nothing in the passage suggests ranking is at stake. ✗
  • Choice D — 'discern': "discern steps" means to perceive or make out evolutionary steps — exactly what analyzing fossil remains allows scientists to do. This pairs naturally with "illuminate" as both verbs describe gaining understanding. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
